* fix: Three.js GPU memory leaks + production checklist 259-267 audit

## Fix (262) — Three.js WebGL memory cleanup
- SceneBuilder.tsx: scene.clone() was called on every render of the Model
  component — memoize with useMemo([scene]) so clone happens once per
  scene reference, not every re-render
- Add useEffect cleanup in Model that traverses the cloned scene on
  unmount and calls geometry.dispose() + material.dispose() on every
  THREE.Mesh to release GPU memory
- handleClear() now revokes blob: URLs created by URL.createObjectURL()
  before clearing the assets array — prevents orphaned browser memory

## Audit (252, 254, 258–261, 266–267) — mark already-implemented items
- 250/254: injection blocking + Electron contextIsolation — done last commit
- 252: pnpm audit --audit-level=high already in deploy.yml (line 48-51)
- 258: src/lib/auditLogChain.ts already implements full SHA-256 hash chain
- 259: getFirebasePerf() lazy singleton already in firebase.ts
- 260: src/lib/webVitals.ts already reports onCLS/onINP/onLCP/onFCP/onTTFB
- 261: 15MB JS budget CI gate already in deploy.yml (lines 111-130)
- 266: onSnapshot cleanup confirmed correct across all hooks and services
- 267: firebase.json already has max-age=31536000,immutable for assets
  and no-cache for HTML entry points

* feat(253): Firestore security rules unit tests

Adds Firebase Emulator-based rules tests to prevent security regressions
when firestore.rules changes.

- src/test/security/firestore.rules.test.ts: 10 test suites, 32 assertions
  - Unauthenticated access denied across all collections
  - Owner-only access for user docs and subcollections
  - Cross-user access denial
  - Anonymous users blocked from commercial ops (ddexReleases, licenses, orgs)
  - Organization deletion hard-blocked (allow delete: if false)
  - Tax profile deletion hard-blocked (compliance record retention)
  - ISRC update/delete hard-blocked (permanent identifiers)
  - Rate-limit docId regex enforcement (userId_[0-9]+)
  - Finance collections (revenue) owner-only
  - Deny-all: unlisted collections

- vitest.rules.config.ts: Separate Vitest config with node environment
  (rules tests require Node, not jsdom, for @firebase/rules-unit-testing)

- package.json: Added "test:rules" script
  FIRESTORE_EMULATOR_HOST=localhost:8080 vitest run --config vitest.rules.config.ts

fix: add optimistic locking to pipeline and deploy store updates

PipelineUpdate and DeployUpdate now include `AND updated_at=$prev` in
the WHERE clause. If the row was modified between read and write, the
update affects 0 rows and returns ErrStaleUpdate. Store.Update retries
up to 3 times by re-reading and re-applying the callback.

loop + CLI command) modify the same pipeline row.

commit.message:
enhance: [loon] move storage v3 deltalog files under basePath/_delta/ to align with loon convention (#48150)

Related to #44956

Previously, V3 deltalogs were written to
{rootPath}/delta_log/{collID}/{partID}/{segID}/{logID}, separate from
the segment's basePath. The manifest used complex "../" relative paths
to bridge the two locations. This change writes V3 deltalogs directly to
{basePath}/_delta/{logID}, aligning with the C loon library's native
_delta/ convention and simplifying the manifest relative path to just
the logID filename. Legacy V1 segments and existing manifests remain
backward compatible.

Signed-off-by: Congqi Xia <congqi.xia@zilliz.com>

commit.message:
feat(ui-server): incremental HMR manifest updates [#991] (#1109)

Add updateManifest() and deleteManifest() to createVertzBunPlugin() for
incremental manifest regeneration during HMR. The file watcher calls
updateManifest() before SSR re-import so the compiler's manifest view
stays current.

Key behaviors:
- Change detection: compare old vs new manifest shape to skip
  unnecessary cache invalidation
- Cache invalidation: manifestsRecord nulled only when shape changes
- Debug logging: VERTZ_DEBUG=manifest logs hmr-update/hmr-delete events
- Diagnostics: recordManifestUpdate() tracks HMR manifest updates

Known limitation (by design): dependent files are NOT automatically
recompiled when an import's reactivity shape changes during HMR.
They get the updated manifest on next full page refresh.

fix(ui): add LRU eviction to MemoryCache (#1036) (#1108)

* fix(ui): add LRU eviction to MemoryCache to prevent unbounded growth (#1036)

MemoryCache now accepts an optional maxSize (default 1000) and evicts
least-recently-used entries when the limit is exceeded. get() promotes
entries to most-recently-used via Map re-insertion. Uses has() check
to correctly handle falsy cached values (null, 0, false).

SSR caches are unaffected — they are per-request and garbage-collected
at request end. Users can pass maxSize: Infinity to disable eviction.

Closes #1036

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ui): disable LRU eviction for SSR caches

SSR caches are per-request and garbage-collected at request end.
LRU eviction during SSR two-pass render could drop pass-1 data
before pass-2 reads it. Use maxSize: Infinity for SSR caches.

and size > NaN is always false). Fall back to default 1000 when NaN.
Add tests for NaN, empty-string key eviction, and default maxSize
boundary. Add inline comment explaining SSR Infinity maxSize.
